title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The kubelet service file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the kubelet service in the worker node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Run the following command to list the user and group for the kubelet.service file on each node: for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service done OR for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /host/usr/lib/systemd/system/kubelet.service done Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.default_value: By default, OpenShift sets the default user and group for the kubelet.service file to root:root . Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Connect to each node:** Use the following command to connect via the OpenShift CLI to each worker node:  
   ```bash
   oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'
   ```
   This command lists all nodes in the OpenShift cluster.

2. **Check kubelet service file permissions:** For each node, run the command to check the ownership of the kubelet service file:  
   ```bash
   oc debug node/<node-name> -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service
   ```
   You can also check the alternate possible location by using:  
   ```bash
   oc debug node/<node-name> -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /host/usr/lib/systemd/system/kubelet.service
   ```

3. **Validate ownership output:** Verify that the output of the command returns `root:root`. This indicates that the ownership settings are secure and compliant with the control.

4. **Confirm compliance:** If the ownership of the kubelet service file is set to `root:root`, then compliance is confirmed. If it is different, note it for remediation.

**Expected secure configuration:** The ownership of the kubelet service file should be set to `root:root`.

**How to confirm compliance:** By running the above commands and ensuring the output reflects `root:root`, you will confirm compliance with the control. If any node does not meet this expectation, appropriate steps for corrective actions should be initiated.
